mnemonics: PACE Yourself for Secure Computation: P - Privacy (Homomorphic Encryption), A - Accuracy (Differential Privacy), C - Correctness (Zero-Knowledge Proofs), E - Efficiency (Algorithm Complexity)
keyPoints: Homomorphic encryption allows computations to be performed directly on encrypted data without needing to decrypt it first.
Differential privacy provides a mathematical guarantee that the removal or addition of a single database item will not significantly affect the outcome of an analysis, protecting individual records.
The computational complexity and performance overhead of encryption algorithms are critical factors in designing practical data security systems.
commonMistakes: Assuming that encrypted data is unusable for computation, overlooking homomorphic encryption techniques.
Equating data anonymization (which can often be reversed) with differential privacy (which provides a mathematical guarantee of privacy).
Underestimating the performance impact and resource requirements of implementing strong cryptographic protocols in a system.
